findings.description: Multiple h1 headings used and heading hierarchy is inconsistent. The document uses h1 for the title 'ESI Networking & Outreach' which is appropriate, but then nearly all subsequent section headings are h2, including sub-sections like 'Webinars & Trainings' that are under the 'Year in Review: 2025 Recap' section. The Program Advancement sub-levels (Partner, Rising Steward, Steward) correctly use h3, but the overall structure is flat.
findings.evidence: PDF has clear hierarchical structure: 'Year in Review: 2025 Recap' is a major section containing 'Webinars & Trainings', 'Steward Forum', 'Regulatory Roundtable', 'Networking Events', 'Regional Networking' as sub-sections. Similarly 'Looking Ahead: 2026 Events' contains 'Returning Events', 'Internal Auditor Training', etc. All are rendered as h2 in HTML.

findings.description: Section transition slides from the PDF are duplicated as both standalone headings and repeated within subsequent content. 'Year in Review: 2025 Recap' appears as both a standalone h2 heading (from page 3) and is referenced again. 'Looking Ahead: 2026 Events' similarly appears twice - once as a section divider and once as a heading.
findings.evidence: HTML has 'Year in Review: 2025 Recap' as an h2 heading after the collage image, which comes from the transition slide on page 3. The content from page 3 is essentially a title-only page that is reproduced separately from the subsequent content pages. Same pattern for 'Looking Ahead: 2026 Events' and 'Staying Connected: ESI Communications'.

findings.description: Some alt text descriptions are reasonable but could be more specific. Most alt text is descriptive and appropriate for the images shown in the PDF slides.
findings.evidence: Alt text like 'Two people looking at a binder during a training session' for the Webinars & Trainings slide image is accurate. 'Collage of photos from 2025 events' for the Year in Review page is appropriate.

findings.description: The listserv email address appears truncated in the PDF as 'nc-environmentalassistance@lists.deq.nc' and the HTML reproduces this same truncation. The actual address likely ends in '.nc.gov' but both PDF and HTML show the same text, so this is not a conversion error.
findings.evidence: PDF page 16 header shows 'nc-environmentalassistance@lists.deq.nc' and HTML shows the same. This is faithful reproduction.
